title: Sealed sender: hide message origin from relay
description:
Hide the sender's identity from the relay. Currently every Deliver carries from: pub_id — the relay knows the full social graph. With sealed sender, the relay learns only who receives a message, never who sent it. The recipient verifies the sender after decryption.

## Mechanism (Signal-style sealed sender via HPKE)

### Key setup
Each user publishes an HPKE public key alongside their MLS KeyPackage. The HPKE key is separate from the Ed25519 signing key (key separation invariant — see CLAUDE.md). This key is already planned for MLS key exchange.

### Sending a sealed message
Instead of:
  Broadcast { payload: <MLS ciphertext> }

Client sends:
  SealedBroadcast { sealed: <opaque bytes> }

Where sealed = HPKE-Seal(recipient_group_hpke_key, plaintext={sender_pub_id || MLS_ciphertext})

For the single-room broadcast case, the 'recipient' is a well-known room HPKE key (a group key shared via MLS, or a relay-published ephemeral key for the room).

For DMs (Whisper):
  SealedWhisper { to: pub_id, sealed: <HPKE-Seal(recipient_hpke_key, {sender_pub_id || payload})> }

### Relay behavior
- Relay forwards SealedBroadcast as SealedDeliver { sealed: <opaque> } — no from field
- Relay forwards SealedWhisper as SealedWhisperDeliver { sealed: <opaque> } — no from field
- Relay cannot read sealed; cannot learn sender

### Recipient verification
Recipient decrypts sealed with their HPKE private key, obtains {sender_pub_id, payload}.
Verifies sender_pub_id is a known peer (from directory).
MLS epoch key implicitly authenticates that a group member sent the outer MLS ciphertext.

### Transition
- Introduce SealedBroadcast / SealedDeliver alongside existing Broadcast / Deliver
- Relay supports both during transition; old clients use Deliver (from visible), new clients use SealedDeliver (from hidden)
- Deprecate unsealed variants once all clients upgrade

## What the relay still learns
- Who is online (connection presence)
- When messages are sent and received
- Message sizes (mitigated by padding — separate epic)
- For DMs: who receives a message (the 'to' field in SealedWhisper is still visible)

## What the relay no longer learns
- Who sent any message
- The social graph of who talks to whom

## Dependencies
- nie-t4t (MLS Phase 1) — HPKE keys are provisioned via MLS KeyPackage infrastructure
- nie-hlns (JSON-RPC migration) — new SealedBroadcast/SealedDeliver message types added to the updated protocol
